name: "New Unit Draft"
about: Claim a unit and track authoring it from Planned to Draft
title: "[New Unit] <UNIT CODE> — <Unit Title>"
labels: ["unit: new-draft", "status: planned"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
Use this issue to CLAIM a unit before you start writing, so two contributors
don't draft the same one. See docs/contributor-onboarding.md for the full process
and docs/unit-assignments.md for the live assignment register.
-->

## Unit

- **Unit code:** <!-- e.g. TH02 -->
- **Unit title:** <!-- e.g. Network Threat Hunting -->
- **Degree layer:** <!-- Foundation / Operational Core / Strategic Core / Major / Capstone -->
- **Target file path:** <!-- e.g. degrees/operational/threat-hunting/th02-...-/README.md -->

## Author

- **Claiming author (GitHub handle):**
- **Domain expertise relevant to this unit:**

## Plan

- [ ] Copied `templates/unit-template.md` to the target path
- [ ] Drafted overview, learning outcomes (correct Bloom's level for the layer)
- [ ] Wrote >= 6 substantive topics
- [ ] Wrote >= 2 complete labs (free/OSS for Foundation/Core)
- [ ] Wrote formative + summative assessment with a 4-level rubric
- [ ] Completed framework mappings (>= 2, incl. 1 Australian / ASD)
- [ ] Added Australian context and >= 5 annotated references (>= 1 Australian)
- [ ] Completed the Unit Metadata table
- [ ] Ran `python3 .github/scripts/lint_units.py <file>` locally and it passes

## Notes

<!-- Anything reviewers should know, dependencies on other units, open questions. -->
